samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, and green outputs, controlled by a pedestrian request button. The traffic light operates in a cycle of 60 clock cycles green, 5 clock cycles yellow, and 10 clock cycles red. If the pedestrian button is pressed while green, the green duration is reduced to 10 cycles if currently more than 10 cycles remain; otherwise, it remains unchanged. The outputs include the current state of the traffic light and a countdown timer.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1: Red light.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2: Yellow light.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3: Green light.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing.</reg>
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the traffic light controller.</reg>
        <reg id="p_red">Previous red signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_yellow">Previous yellow signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_green">Previous green signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if (pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if (!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if (!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign output signals
        assign clock = cnt;
        
        // Output logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                red <= 0;
                yellow <= 0;
                green <= 0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
